title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud services. By exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines, attackers can deploy malicious code, potentially leading to service disruptions, data breaches, and financial losses.
recommended_actions: Conduct an immediate review of CI/CD pipelines to identify and remediate any unauthorized access or vulnerabilities.
Implement robust monitoring and logging for cloud environments to detect anomalous activities and respond quickly to potential threats.
Ensure regular backups of critical data are performed and stored securely offline to facilitate recovery in case of a ransomware attack.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 10.11.x <= 10.11.13, 11.4.x <= 11.4.3 fail to sanitize...
description: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 10.11.x <= 10.11.13, 11.4.x <= 11.4.3 fail to sanitize sensitive configuration fields in the Mattermost Calls plugin which allows an attacker with access to a support packet to obtain TURN server credentials via the plaintext values present in the exported plugin configuration.. Mattermost Advisory ID: MMSA-2026-00605

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Mattermost allows attackers with access to support packets to extract TURN server credentials in plaintext, potentially leading to unauthorized access to communication systems and sensitive data leakage.
recommended_actions: Upgrade Mattermost to the latest patched version that addresses CVE-2026-6347.
Restrict access to support packets to authorized personnel only and review access controls.
Rotate TURN server credentials immediately to mitigate potential unauthorized access.

title: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1 fail to verify channel membership when processing AI...
description: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1 fail to verify channel membership when processing AI-assisted message rewrites which allows an authenticated attacker to read the content of threads in private channels and direct messages they do not have access to via a crafted request to the post rewrite endpoint.. Mattermost Advisory ID: MMSA-2026-00645

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-5163.

title: Improper neutralization of input during web page generation ('cross-site scripting')...
description:
Improper neutralization of input during web page generation ('cross-site scripting') vulnerability in Basamak Information Technology Consulting and Organization Trade Ltd. Co. DernekWeb allows Stored XSS.

This issue affects DernekWeb: through 30122025.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7498.

title: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 10.11.x <= 10.11.13, 11.4.x <= 11.4.3 fail to check public...
description: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 10.11.x <= 10.11.13, 11.4.x <= 11.4.3 fail to check public/private permissions which allows members without these permissions to access public playbooks via /get.. Mattermost Advisory ID: MMSA-2026-00591

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6343.

title: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 11.4.x <= 11.4.3 fail to validate the X-Requested-With...
description: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 11.4.x <= 11.4.3 fail to validate the X-Requested-With header on the burn-on-read reveal endpoint which allows an authenticated channel member to force the reveal of a burn-on-read message without recipient consent via a crafted Markdown image tag.. Mattermost Advisory ID: MMSA-2026-00636

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6339.

title: Mattermost Desktop App versions <=6.1 6.0.1 5.4.13.0 fail to prevent server-rendered content from...
description: Mattermost Desktop App versions <=6.1 6.0.1 5.4.13.0 fail to prevent server-rendered content from closing an underlying application view in the Mattermost Desktop App which allows a malicious server or plugin to crash the desktop client via invoking {{window.close()}} in the renderer context, leading to a denial of service condition at the client level. Mattermost Advisory ID: MMSA-2026-00633

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows a malicious server or plugin to crash the Mattermost Desktop App by invoking window.close() in the renderer context, resulting in a denial of service condition. However, the impact is limited to client-side disruption without compromising sensitive data or system integrity.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to the latest version of the Mattermost Desktop App that addresses this vulnerability.
Restrict the use of untrusted plugins and ensure all plugins are sourced from verified providers.
Monitor and audit server configurations to prevent malicious server-rendered content.

title: Mattermost Plugins versions <=11.5 11.1.5 10.13.11 11.3.4.0 fail to properly check for...
description: Mattermost Plugins versions <=11.5 11.1.5 10.13.11 11.3.4.0 fail to properly check for permissions when processing commands in the Gitlab plugin which allows normal users to uninstall instances or setup webhook connections via the {{gitlab instance {option}}} or the {{/gitlab webhook {option}}} commands. Mattermost Advisory ID: MMSA-2026-00600

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-3117.

title: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 10.11.x <= 10.11.13 fail to validate the Host header when...
description: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 10.11.x <= 10.11.13 fail to validate the Host header when constructing response URLs for custom slash commands which allows an authenticated attacker to redirect slash command responses to an attacker-controlled server via a spoofed Host header.. Mattermost Advisory ID: MMSA-2026-00582

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an authenticated attacker to exploit improper validation of the Host header in specific Mattermost versions, potentially redirecting slash command responses to an attacker-controlled server. This could lead to data leakage or unauthorized access to sensitive information.
recommended_actions: Upgrade Mattermost to the latest patched version that addresses this vulnerability.
Review and monitor logs for any unusual activity related to slash commands or Host header manipulation.
Implement additional security measures such as strict validation of Host headers at the application or web server level.

title: Mattermost Plugins versions <=11.5 11.1.5 10.13.11 11.3.4.0 fail to have API-level checks on...
description: Mattermost Plugins versions <=11.5 11.1.5 10.13.11 11.3.4.0 fail to have API-level checks on which groups the user can create issues or attach comments to which allows a user that is member of multiple groups to create issues to a locked group via direct API requests. Mattermost Advisory ID: MMSA-2026-00602

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6341.

title: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 10.11.x <= 10.11.13, 11.4.x <= 11.4.3 fail prevent...
description: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 10.11.x <= 10.11.13, 11.4.x <= 11.4.3 fail prevent disclosure of created user password which allows a malicious attacker to impersonate a user via the use of some of those passwords.. Mattermost Advisory ID: MMSA-2026-00614

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Mattermost allows attackers to potentially obtain user passwords, enabling unauthorized access and impersonation of users. This could lead to data breaches, unauthorized actions, and compromise of sensitive information.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Mattermost to the latest patched version to mitigate the vulnerability.
Conduct a review of user accounts and enforce a password reset for all users to prevent unauthorized access.
Enable and enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) to add an additional layer of security to user accounts.

title: Mattermost Desktop App versions <=6.1 6.0.1 5.4.13.0 fail to prevent an invalid URL from loading...
description: Mattermost Desktop App versions <=6.1 6.0.1 5.4.13.0 fail to prevent an invalid URL from loading in a pop-up window in the Mattermost Desktop App which allows a malicious server owner to repeated crash the application via calling {{window.open('javascript:alert()');}}. Mattermost Advisory ID: MMSA-2026-00618

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows a malicious server owner to crash the Mattermost Desktop App repeatedly, potentially disrupting communication workflows and productivity. It may also serve as a vector for further exploitation if combined with other vulnerabilities.
recommended_actions: Upgrade Mattermost Desktop App to the latest version that addresses CVE-2026-3471.
Implement network-level controls to restrict access to untrusted or malicious servers.
Educate users about avoiding suspicious links and servers while using the application.

title: Mattermost Plugins versions <=11.5 11.1.5 10.13.11 11.3.4.0 fail to appropriately check for valid...
description: Mattermost Plugins versions <=11.5 11.1.5 10.13.11 11.3.4.0 fail to appropriately check for valid namespaces which allows plugin users to create subscriptions to groups that were not whitelisted via creating groups that share the same prefix as a whitelisted group. Mattermost Advisory ID: MMSA-2026-00601

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6342.

title: A vulnerability in Command-Line Client in P4 Server prior to the 2025.2 Patch 2, identified as...
description: A vulnerability in Command-Line Client in P4 Server prior to the 2025.2 Patch 2, identified as CVE-2026-6902, has been fixed in P4 Server to address potential security risks.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6902.

title: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 10.11.x <= 10.11.13, 11.4.x <= 11.4.3 fail to sanitize...
description: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 10.11.x <= 10.11.13, 11.4.x <= 11.4.3 fail to sanitize sensitive configuration fields before including them in support packet generation, which allows a Mattermost System Admin or any party with access to a support packet to obtain sensitive credentials in plaintext via downloading a support packet from the System Console.. Mattermost Advisory ID: MMSA-2026-00607

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows sensitive credentials to be exposed in plaintext within support packets generated by Mattermost. This could lead to unauthorized access to critical systems, data breaches, and potential compromise of user accounts if the support packets are accessed by malicious actors.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Mattermost to the latest patched version that addresses this vulnerability.
Restrict access to the System Console and generated support packets to authorized personnel only.
Review and rotate any credentials that may have been exposed in previously generated support packets.

title: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 10.11.x <= 10.11.13, 11.4.x <= 11.4.3 Fail to enforce slash...
description: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 10.11.x <= 10.11.13, 11.4.x <= 11.4.3 Fail to enforce slash command trigger-word uniqueness during command updates which allows an authenticated team member with Manage Own Slash Commands permission to hijack and impersonate existing system or custom slash commands via editing their own slash command trigger to an already-registered trigger through the command update API. Mattermost Advisory ID: MMSA-2026-00597

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-28732.

title: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 10.11.x <= 10.11.13 fail to check if {{team_id}} was being...
description: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 10.11.x <= 10.11.13 fail to check if {{team_id}} was being changed when updating playbooks, allowing users with only {{Manage Playbook Configurations}} permission to change a playbook's team, bypassing manage members restriction via PUT api. Mattermost Advisory ID: MMSA-2025-00552

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows users with 'Manage Playbook Configurations' permissions to bypass team membership restrictions and change a playbook's team. This could lead to unauthorized access or manipulation of playbooks, potentially exposing sensitive operational data or disrupting workflows.
recommended_actions: Upgrade Mattermost to the latest patched versions that address CVE-2026-4286.
Restrict 'Manage Playbook Configurations' permissions to trusted users only.
Monitor and audit playbook changes to detect any unauthorized modifications.

title:
Net::Statsd::Lite versions through 0.10.0 for Perl allowed metric injections.

The values from...
description:
Net::Statsd::Lite versions through 0.10.0 for Perl allowed metric injections.

The values from the set_add method were not checked for newlines, colons or pipes. Metrics generated from untrusted sources could inject additional statsd metrics.

Note that version 0.9.0 fixed a similar issue CVE-2026-46719 for metric names.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8788.

title: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 10.11.x <= 10.11.13 fail to validate that the...
description: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 10.11.x <= 10.11.13 fail to validate that the RefreshedToken differs from the original invite token during remote cluster invite confirmation which allows an authenticated attacker to bypass token rotation and reuse the original invite token via sending a crafted invite confirmation with a RefreshedToken matching the original token. Mattermost Advisory ID: MMSA-2026-00575

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4273.

title: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 10.11.x <= 10.11.13, 11.4.x <= 11.4.3 fail to validate 7zip...
description: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 10.11.x <= 10.11.13, 11.4.x <= 11.4.3 fail to validate 7zip archive structure before processing which allows an authenticated attacker to cause server memory exhaustion and denial of service via uploading a specially crafted 7zip file with excessive folder declarations.. Mattermost Advisory ID: MMSA-2026-00573

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6340.

title: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 10.11.x <= 10.11.13 fail to enforce client identity binding...
description: Mattermost versions 11.5.x <= 11.5.1, 10.11.x <= 10.11.13 fail to enforce client identity binding during the OAuth authorization code redemption flow which allows an authenticated OAuth client to redeem authorization codes issued to a different client via a crafted token exchange request.. Mattermost Advisory ID: MMSA-2026-00570

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Mattermost allows an authenticated OAuth client to redeem authorization codes issued to a different client, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive data or services. However, the severity is rated as low due to the requirement for an already authenticated client and specific conditions for exploitation.
recommended_actions: Upgrade Mattermost to a patched version higher than 11.5.1 or 10.11.13 to mitigate the vulnerability.
Review and monitor OAuth client configurations and authorization flows for any unusual activity.
Educate administrators and developers on secure OAuth implementation practices to prevent similar issues.

title: CVE-2026-8266 - A vulnerability was detected in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. This affects the function gsm_build_pdu_session
description: A vulnerability was detected in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. This affects the function gsm_build_pdu_session_establishment_accept of the file /src/smf/gsm-build.c of the component SMF. The manipulation results in denial of service. The attack can be launched remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8266.

title: CVE-2026-8265 - A security vulnerability has been detected in Tenda AC6 15.03.06.23. Affected by this issue is the f
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in Tenda AC6 15.03.06.23. Affected by this issue is the function get_log_file of the file /goform/getLogFile of the component httpd. The manipulation of the argument wans.flag leads to os command injection. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8265.

title: CVE-2026-8264 - A weakness has been identified in Tenda AC6 15.03.06.23. Affected by this vulnerability is the funct
description: A weakness has been identified in Tenda AC6 15.03.06.23. Affected by this vulnerability is the function formWifiApScan of the file /goform/WifiApScan of the component httpd. Executing a manipulation of the argument wl2g.public.country/wl5g.public.country can lead to os command injection. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to execute OS command injection, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or complete system compromise of affected devices. This could be exploited to launch further attacks within a network.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the firmware of Tenda AC6 devices to the latest version if a patch is available. Contact the vendor for confirmation.
Implement network segmentation to isolate vulnerable devices from critical systems and limit exposure to external networks.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and apply intrusion detection/prevention systems to identify potential exploitation attempts.

title: CVE-2026-8263 - A security flaw has been discovered in Tenda AC6 15.03.06.49_multi_TDE01. Affected is the function f
description: A security flaw has been discovered in Tenda AC6 15.03.06.49_multi_TDE01. Affected is the function fromSetWirelessRepeat of the file /goform/WifiExtraSet of the component httpd. Performing a manipulation of the argument mac/ssid results in os command injection. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8263.

title: CVE-2026-8261 - A vulnerability was determined in Squirrel up to 3.2. This affects the function SQFunctionProto::Loa
description: A vulnerability was determined in Squirrel up to 3.2. This affects the function SQFunctionProto::Load of the file squirrel/sqobject.cpp. This manipulation causes heap-based buffer overflow. The attack is restricted to local execution. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8261.

title: CVE-2026-8260 - A vulnerability was found in D-Link DCS-935L up to 1.10.01. The impacted element is the function Set
description: A vulnerability was found in D-Link DCS-935L up to 1.10.01. The impacted element is the function SetDeviceSettings of the file /web/cgi-bin/hnap/hnap_service of the component HNAP Service. The manipulation of the argument AdminPassword results in buffer overflow. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8260.

title: CVE-2026-8259 - A vulnerability has been found in Tenda AC6 2.0/15.03.06.23. The affected element is an unknown func
description: A vulnerability has been found in Tenda AC6 2.0/15.03.06.23. The affected element is an unknown function of the file /goform/telnet of the component httpd. The manipulation of the argument lan.ip leads to os command injection. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used.

title: CVE-2026-8258 - A flaw has been found in Squirrel up to 3.2. Impacted is the function validate_format in the library
description: A flaw has been found in Squirrel up to 3.2. Impacted is the function validate_format in the library sqstdlib/sqstdstring.cpp. Executing a manipulation can lead to stack-based buffer overflow. The attack can only be executed locally. The exploit has been published and may be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8258.

title: CVE-2026-8257 - A vulnerability was detected in WebAssembly Binaryen up to 117. This issue affects the function IRBu
description: A vulnerability was detected in WebAssembly Binaryen up to 117. This issue affects the function IRBuilder::makeBrOn of the file src/wasm/wasm-ir-builder.cpp of the component BrOn Parser. Performing a manipulation results in reachable assertion. The attack needs to be approached locally. The exploit is now public and may be used. The patch is named 1251efbc1ea471c1311d2726b2bbe061ff2a291c. It is suggested to install a patch to address this issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8257.
